title: A5: lifecycle delegation (resume/close/exit/restart) via server registry
description: CRITICAL per Codex review. title: A0: split TerminalTypePlugin into server/client plugin + two registries
description: CRITICAL per Codex review. Split src/types/terminal-type.ts: TerminalTypeServerPlugin (createSession, onSessionExit, onSessionRestart, onSessionClose, validateInput, useTmux/shell config) — server-safe, no React imports. TerminalTypeClientPlugin (component: React.ComponentType<{session, props}>, icon, title) — client-only. Two separate registries. session-service imports only server registry. Rationale: plugins today mix Lucide icons + client modals with server config, which would bundle client code into server if we routed session-service through the combined registry.

title: A3: extend CreateSessionInput with generic typeMetadata
description: Add typeMetadata?: Record<string, unknown> to CreateSessionInput in src/types/session.ts. Route it through the POST /api/sessions handler and SessionService. notes: REVISED per Codex: in addition to generic typeMetadata, add a first-class scope_key column to terminal_session (indexed, nullable). Server-side dedup in POST /api/sessions. Client-side dedup-by-list scan is unreliable.

title: A1: route session-service through plugin.createSession()

Add test coverage for plugin dispatch.
notes: CRITICAL per Codex review. Only after A0 is landed (server registry exists). session-service must not import client plugins.

title: A2: route SessionManager render through plugin.renderContent()
description: Refactor SessionManager.tsx lines 1593-1727 to look up the plugin via registry and call plugin.renderContent(session, props) instead of the file/loop/browser/shell switch. Keep the key={session.id} remount semantics. Update TerminalTypeRenderer.tsx similarly or delete if redundant.
notes: REVISED per Codex: do NOT call plugin.renderContent() — replace switch with a client-side component registry lookup (clientRegistry.get(type).component returns React.ComponentType). Hooks belong in components, not plugin methods. Remove renderContent() from plugin interface in A0 split.

title: Couple projects strictly to folders
description: Major refactor: strictly couple folder and project. Parent folders become groupings only; child folders are projects. Remove decoupled project abstraction.
design: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-20-project-folder-refactor-master.md — master plan with all shared decisions (naming, hierarchy, ownership matrix, groups-as-active rollup, migration strategy, risk register).
notes:
DECISIONS (2026-04-20):
- Hierarchy: Groups nestable (any depth), Projects are leaves only
- Ownership: Groups hold preferences only. Projects own sessions, tasks, channels, secrets, git, profile, account binding
- Migration: Auto-classify by depth + contents. Leaf folders -> projects; non-leaf -> groups. Sessions on group-like folders move to auto-created 'Default' project
- Naming: Full rename. New tables project_group + project. Types Project/Group. APIs /api/projects + /api/groups. FK renames (folderId -> projectId on task/channel/etc)
Master plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-20-project-folder-refactor-master.md (updated with codex review findings: forward-only cutover policy, Section 6a column appendix, 8-risk register, phase-specific integration tests)
close_reason: All 6 phases complete: domain+application (NodeRef/ProjectGroup/Project), services+API (repos, group-service, project-service, node-preferences routes), UI (ProjectTreeContext, GroupPreferencesModal, ProjectPreferencesModal, sidebar), rdv CLI (group/project subcommands), cleanup (legacy tables dropped, folder_id removed, docs refreshed). typecheck clean (baseline); 314/315 tests pass; cargo build + 11 rdv CLI tests green.

title: Terminal: tmux status bar still shows on pre-existing sessions (status off not applied on attach)
description: createTmuxSession sets 'status off' at creation time. attachToTmuxSession does not re-apply it, so pre-existing tmux sessions (created before the April 10 fix or where the option was not persisted) keep the status bar on, covering the bottom row of terminal content. Fix: apply 'status off' in attachToTmuxSession so every attach is guaranteed to have status bar disabled. Same fix should apply 'aggressive-resize off' defensively too.

title: Terminal: bottom row clipped after resize (WebGL cell-height overshoot)
description: After the settle-and-fit fix (#181), the very last row is still clipped on resize — visible in Claude Code's bottom mode indicator. Caused by xterm's FitAddon computing rows with a fractional cellHeight that the WebGL renderer rounds up when painting, so N rows overflow container height by subpixels. Fix: after fit(), check if rendered screenElement height exceeds container height; if so, terminal.resize(cols, rows-1).

title: Convert content-rich modals into Terminal Types (epic)
description:
Convert IssuesModal, PRsModal, RecordingsModal, ProfilesModal, UserSettingsModal into first-class terminal types that render as full workspace tabs. Parent epic covering infrastructure refactor + 5 plugin implementations + entry-point rewiring + cleanup.

Motivation: Dialog modals clip content horizontally (see remote-dev-lb3m) and cramp information-dense views with inner nav. Terminal tabs give full workspace real estate, live alongside terminals, and persist across reloads like other sessions.

Scope boundaries:
- IN: Issues, PRs, Profiles, Settings, Recordings
- OUT: Lightweight action modals (delete confirms, save forms, keyboard shortcuts panel, etc.)
- OUT: New top-level UI buttons (context menu entry retained)

Design choices already settled with user:
- Per-project dedup: one tab per (terminalType, scopeKey)
- Full plugin refactor: plugin.createSession + plugin.renderContent become live, not dead code

title: Write integration tests for environment persistence
description:
Create integration tests to verify environment persistence across scenarios.

Files: test/integration/environment-persistence.test.ts (NEW)

Test Scenarios:

1. Environment survives shell exit:
   - Create session with profile env
   - Exit shell (send 'exit' command)
   - Reconnect to session
   - Verify environment variables still present

2. Agent restart preserves environment:
   - Create agent session with API keys
   - Send agent exit (or crash it)
   - Restart agent via restart_agent handler
   - Verify API keys still in environment

3. Profile isolation works without breaking HOME:
   - Create session with profile
   - Verify user's .bashrc loaded (echo $BASH_VERSION)
   - Verify CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR points to profile
   - Verify HOME still points to real home

4. Runtime port conflicts detected:
   - Start a server on port 3000
   - Create session with PORT=3000
   - Verify runtime conflict warning returned

5. Environment stack merges correctly:
   - Create folder env with VAR1=folder
   - Create profile with VAR1=profile, VAR2=profile
   - Verify VAR1=folder (folder wins), VAR2=profile

Test Infrastructure:
- Use real tmux sessions (not mocked)
- Create/cleanup sessions in beforeEach/afterEach
- Use test-specific session names (rdv-test-*)

Tests: This IS the test task, should pass when done

title: Create RestartAgentUseCase
description:
Create a use case for restarting agent sessions with environment preservation.

File: src/application/use-cases/session/RestartAgentUseCase.ts

Responsibilities:
- Restart agent CLI without losing environment
- Leverage tmux environment persistence (no re-injection needed if tmux session preserved)
- If tmux session recreated, re-inject environment via EnvironmentManager
- Update agent state in repository
- Send new agent command via tmux gateway

Flow:
1. Load session from repository
2. Mark as restarting, save
3. If tmux session exists, just send new command (env persists)
4. If tmux session gone, recreate with environment from EnvironmentManager
5. Mark as running, save

Interface:
export class RestartAgentUseCase {
  constructor(
    sessionRepo: SessionRepository,
    tmuxGateway: TmuxGateway,
    environmentManager: EnvironmentManager
  )
  
  async execute(sessionId: string, userId: string): Promise<Session>;
}

title: Create ProfileIsolation value object
description:
Create a value object for XDG-compliant profile isolation WITHOUT overriding HOME.

File: src/domain/value-objects/ProfileIsolation.ts

Responsibilities:
- Generate XDG environment variables for profile isolation
- Preserve user's real HOME (don't override)
- Generate agent-specific config paths (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR, CODEX_HOME, etc.)
- Generate GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL path
- Validate SSH key paths for GIT_SSH_COMMAND

Interface:
- static create(profileDir: string, realHome: string): ProfileIsolation
- toEnvironment(): TmuxEnvironment
- getConfigHome(): string
- getDataHome(): string  
- getCacheHome(): string
- getGitConfigPath(): string

The key insight: Use XDG_CONFIG_HOME, XDG_DATA_HOME, XDG_CACHE_HOME instead of HOME override. This lets agents use isolated configs while user's dotfiles (.bashrc, .zshrc) still load from real HOME.
